learnings.content: Always use Server Components by default in Next.js App Router; only add 'use client' when you need browser APIs, event handlers, or React hooks.
Use server actions for form mutations. Never call API routes directly from client components when a server action can do the job.
Use Zod for all runtime validation at form boundaries. Never trust unvalidated input from client components.
Co-locate loading.tsx and error.tsx with each route segment rather than wrapping everything in a single global error boundary.
Prefer the App Router data-fetching pattern: fetch in Server Components and pass data down as props. Avoid client-side fetching unless you need real-time updates.
Use useOptimistic() for instant UI feedback on mutations instead of manually managing loading state.
Route groups with (groupName) are for layout sharing only. Never use them to control URL paths.
